title: Rich Dense British Lentils Stew
description: A robust, flavourful lentil stew with a subtle hint of fennel, simmered to a rich, dense consistency that warms the soul.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery, sautéing until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, fennel seeds, thyme, smoked paprika, and tomato paste, cooking for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the rinsed lentils, bay leaves, and vegetable stock to the pot, bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 35-40 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the stew has thickened.
Remove the bay leaves and mash a portion of the lentils against the side of the pot to increase density.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh parsley or a drizzle of olive oil.
